items.text: Notice: Refunds for the second-floor vending machine in Harper Hall are issued by the Facilities Office, Room 118, until Friday, March 6. Bring your receipt and the exact amount lost. A maximum of $2.00 will be returned per person. Questions may be directed to Mr. Okafor.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is the largest amount a person can be refunded?
questions.explanation: The notice states that a maximum of $2.00 will be returned per person, so two dollars is the largest amount available.

questions.stem: According to the notice, where should a person go to obtain a refund?

options.A: To the Facilities Office in Room 118.
options.B: To the vending machine on the second floor.
options.C: To the cashier's window in Harper Hall.
options.D: To the student services desk on the first floor.

questions.explanation: The notice says refunds are issued by the Facilities Office, Room 118, which is where a person must go.

items.text:
Hi Devon,

Thanks for signing up as a campus tour guide! Your first shift is confirmed for Saturday, April 11, from 10:00 AM to noon. Please meet at the Visitor Center desk fifteen minutes early to collect your badge and map.

See you then,
Coordinator Ruiz

questions.stem: According to the email, when is Devon's shift scheduled to begin?
questions.explanation: The email confirms the shift runs from 10:00 AM to noon, so it begins at ten in the morning.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the badge and map?

options.A: They are mailed to guides before the shift.
options.B: They are handed out at the Visitor Center.
options.C: They are purchased for a small fee.
options.D: They are shared among all the guides.

questions.explanation: Devon is told to meet at the Visitor Center desk early to collect the badge and map, implying they are distributed there.

items.text: Notice: During spring break, March 16-20, the Ellis Library will operate on reduced hours, opening at 10:00 AM and closing at 4:00 PM daily. The third-floor study rooms will remain closed all week. Normal hours resume Monday, March 23. Questions may be directed to the circulation desk.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what time will the library close during spring break?

options.A: It will close at ten in the morning.
options.B: It will close at six in the evening.
options.C: It will close at noon each day.
options.D: It will close at four in the afternoon.

questions.explanation: The notice states the library closes at 4:00 PM daily during spring break, which is four in the afternoon.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the third-floor study rooms?

options.A: They will be renovated over the break.
options.B: They will open earlier than usual.
options.C: They will be available again on March 23.
options.D: They will require a reservation fee.

questions.explanation: The rooms are closed all break week, but normal hours resume March 23, implying they become available again then.

items.text:
Dear Priya,

My office hours are moved this week only: Thursday, 1:00-3:00 PM, in Room 305. If you'd like a printed copy of the study guide, please bring $3.50 to cover the printing cost. Otherwise, the digital version is free.

Best,
Professor Lane

questions.stem: According to the email, where will office hours be held this week?
questions.explanation: The email states the relocated office hours will be held in Room 305 this week.

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Priya pay for a printed study guide?
questions.explanation: The email asks Priya to bring $3.50 to cover the printing cost of a hard copy, which is three fifty.
